metadata.title: Mobi-lyze Sonification App version 1 (build 27)
metadata.description: Mobi-lyze is a sonification application for historical Tandem Mobi insulin pump data. This software deposit supplements the conference paper Mobi-lyze: A Sonification System for Type-1 Diabetes Management (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.20489587). The software is under active development and is not in a publicly releasable state. This deposit is made for academic record-keeping purposes and to establish a citable artifact associated with the published research. The software is built using Cycling '74's Max environment. Max must be installed for this software to function.

meeting.title: The 31st International Conference on Auditory Display

metadata.title: Fault-Tolerance Scaffold Simulator: Hardware Errors, Measurement Errors, and Software Decoding
metadata.description: A classical repetition-code simulator that introduces the systems logic of fault tolerance through hardware errors, measurement errors, and majority-vote software decoding.
metadata.notes: If you use this software, please cite it as below.

data.title: Fault-Tolerance Scaffold Simulator: Hardware Errors, Measurement Errors, and Software Decoding

metadata.title: mint
metadata.description: Modified Intraneuronal Nanoparticle Tracking (MINT) is a Python script used to extract intraneuronal transport parameters from the trajectories of optically active nanoparticles.
metadata.notes: If you use this software, please cite it as below.

metadata.title: dCache Software
metadata.description: dCache - a system for storing and retrieving huge amounts of data, distributed among a large number of heterogenous server nodes, under a single virtual filesystem tree with a variety of standard access methods
metadata.notes: If you use this software, please cite it as below.

metadata.title: PerezHz/TaylorIntegration.jl: v0.18.9
metadata.description:
TaylorIntegration v0.18.9

Diff since v0.18.8

Some minor fixes in support of https://github.com/PerezHz/NEOs.jl/pull/206, including:

  • Handle empty dense TaylorSolution polynomials (previously returned error, now an empty solution is built)

  • Export DensePropagation1, DensePropagation2

Merged pull requests:

  • Some post #237 fixes (#238) (@PerezHz)

metadata.title: PyEvoc: Computational Hierarchical Evocation Analysis for Digital Corpora
metadata.description: PyEvoc is a research-oriented Python framework for adapting the Hierarchical Evocation Method to large-scale digital textual corpora.
metadata.notes: If you use PyEvoc, please cite this software release.

data.title: PyEvoc: Computational Hierarchical Evocation Analysis for Digital Corpora

metadata.title: jHDF
metadata.description:
• Fix chunk coordinate handling in chunked datasets so retrieval from chunk map succeeds. Thanks to @frothga https://github.com/jamesmudd/jhdf/pull/800 https://github.com/jamesmudd/jhdf/issues/727 and https://github.com/jamesmudd/jhdf/issues/760
  • Add support for Java 25
  • Switch from com.ning:compress-lzf to replacement at.yawk.lz4:lz4-java for LZ4 compression support
  • Enable Gradle dependency locking
  • Fix JitPack configuration for rolling jar releases
  • CI: Skip SonarQube job when SONAR_TOKEN is unavailable
  • Build and dependency updates
metadata.notes: If you use this software, please cite it using the metadata from this file.

metadata.title: MCRA Core
metadata.description: The Monte Carlo Risk Assessment (MCRA) software contains models for human health risk assessment for combined exposure to multiple chemicals via food intake, inhalation and dermal contact. Models are available to address all major areas of risk assessment, including hazard identification, hazard characterisation, (dietary and non-dietary) exposure assessment and risk assessment. The software supports risk assessments for any group of substances, health effect and population. MCRA also contains models following the guidelines and regulatory methodologies of the European Commission and the European Food Safety Authority (EFSA). The MCRA Core library contains the model implementations of the MCRA software that are used in the MCRA Web platform.
